comment_text.sn12.35:5.1: “Twist” (visūka) is used with “dodge” (vipphandita) and sometimes “duck” (visevita) for an horse fighting the bit (MN 65:33.2) or a crab escaping the torment of children (MN 35:23.9). Visūka is also used for a “show” of dance, etc., where the commentarial gloss paṭāṇi (“screw”) reinforces the sense “twist, gyrate”. As descriptions of views, they suggest the active process of denial and distortion through which views shape how we see the world.

root_text.sn12.35:1.5: Evaṁ vutte, aññataro bhikkhu bhagavantaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.sn12.35:1.6: “‘katamaṁ nu kho, bhante, jarāmaraṇaṁ, kassa ca panidaṁ jarāmaraṇan’ti?
root_text.sn12.35:1.7: ‘No kallo pañho’ti bhagavā avoca, ‘katamaṁ jarāmaraṇaṁ, kassa ca panidaṁ jarāmaraṇan’ti iti vā, bhikkhu, yo vadeyya, ‘aññaṁ jarāmaraṇaṁ aññassa ca panidaṁ jarāmaraṇan’ti, iti vā, bhikkhu, yo vadeyya, ubhayametaṁ ekatthaṁ byañjanameva nānaṁ.
root_text.sn12.35:1.8: Taṁ jīvaṁ taṁ sarīranti vā, bhikkhu, diṭṭhiyā sati brahmacariyavāso na hoti.
root_text.sn12.35:1.9: Aññaṁ jīvaṁ aññaṁ sarīranti vā, bhikkhu, diṭṭhiyā sati brahmacariyavāso na hoti.
root_text.sn12.35:1.10: Ete te, bhikkhu, ubho ante anupagamma majjhena tathāgato dhammaṁ deseti:
root_text.sn12.35:2.1: “Katamā nu kho, bhante, jāti, kassa ca panāyaṁ jātī”ti?
root_text.sn12.35:2.2: “No kallo pañho”ti bhagavā avoca, “‘katamā jāti, kassa ca panāyaṁ jātī’ti iti vā, bhikkhu, yo vadeyya, ‘aññā jāti aññassa ca panāyaṁ jātī’ti iti vā, bhikkhu, yo vadeyya, ubhayametaṁ ekatthaṁ byañjanameva nānaṁ.
root_text.sn12.35:2.3: Taṁ jīvaṁ taṁ sarīranti vā, bhikkhu, diṭṭhiyā sati brahmacariyavāso na hoti.
root_text.sn12.35:2.4: Aññaṁ jīvaṁ aññaṁ sarīranti vā, bhikkhu, diṭṭhiyā sati brahmacariyavāso na hoti.
root_text.sn12.35:2.5: Ete te, bhikkhu, ubho ante anupagamma majjhena tathāgato dhammaṁ deseti:
root_text.sn12.35:3.1: “Katamo nu kho, bhante, bhavo, kassa ca panāyaṁ bhavo”ti?
root_text.sn12.35:3.2: “No kallo pañho”ti bhagavā avoca, “‘katamo bhavo, kassa ca panāyaṁ bhavo’ti iti vā, bhikkhu, yo vadeyya, ‘añño bhavo aññassa ca panāyaṁ bhavo’ti iti vā, bhikkhu, yo vadeyya, ubhayametaṁ ekatthaṁ byañjanameva nānaṁ.
root_text.sn12.35:3.3: Taṁ jīvaṁ taṁ sarīranti vā, bhikkhu, diṭṭhiyā sati brahmacariyavāso na hoti;
root_text.sn12.35:3.4: aññaṁ jīvaṁ aññaṁ sarīranti vā, bhikkhu, diṭṭhiyā sati brahmacariyavāso na hoti.
root_text.sn12.35:3.5: Ete te, bhikkhu, ubho ante anupagamma majjhena tathāgato dhammaṁ deseti:
root_text.sn12.35:4.1: “Katame nu kho, bhante, saṅkhārā, kassa ca panime saṅkhārā”ti?
root_text.sn12.35:4.2: “No kallo pañho”ti bhagavā avoca, “‘katame saṅkhārā kassa ca panime saṅkhārā’ti iti vā, bhikkhu, yo vadeyya, ‘aññe saṅkhārā aññassa ca panime saṅkhārā’ti iti vā, bhikkhu, yo vadeyya, ubhayametaṁ ekatthaṁ byañjanameva nānaṁ.
root_text.sn12.35:4.3: Taṁ jīvaṁ taṁ sarīranti vā, bhikkhu, diṭṭhiyā sati brahmacariyavāso na hoti;
root_text.sn12.35:4.4: aññaṁ jīvaṁ aññaṁ sarīranti vā, bhikkhu, diṭṭhiyā sati brahmacariyavāso na hoti.
root_text.sn12.35:4.5: Ete te, bhikkhu, ubho ante anupagamma majjhena tathāgato dhammaṁ deseti:
root_text.sn12.35:5.1: “Avijjāya tveva, bhikkhu, asesavirāganirodhā yānissa tāni visūkāyikāni visevitāni vipphanditāni kānici kānici.
root_text.sn12.35:5.2: ‘Katamaṁ jarāmaraṇaṁ, kassa ca panidaṁ jarāmaraṇaṁ’ iti vā, ‘aññaṁ jarāmaraṇaṁ, aññassa ca panidaṁ jarāmaraṇaṁ’ iti vā, ‘taṁ jīvaṁ taṁ sarīraṁ’ iti vā, ‘aññaṁ jīvaṁ aññaṁ sarīraṁ’ iti vā.
root_text.sn12.35:5.3: Sabbānissa tāni pahīnāni bhavanti ucchinnamūlāni tālāvatthukatāni anabhāvaṅkatāni āyatiṁ anuppādadhammāni.
root_text.sn12.35:6.1: Avijjāya tveva, bhikkhu, asesavirāganirodhā yānissa tāni visūkāyikāni visevitāni vipphanditāni kānici kānici.
root_text.sn12.35:6.2: ‘Katamā jāti, kassa ca panāyaṁ jāti’ iti vā, ‘aññā jāti, aññassa ca panāyaṁ jāti’ iti vā, ‘taṁ jīvaṁ taṁ sarīraṁ’ iti vā, ‘aññaṁ jīvaṁ aññaṁ sarīraṁ’ iti vā.
root_text.sn12.35:6.3: Sabbānissa tāni pahīnāni bhavanti ucchinnamūlāni tālāvatthukatāni anabhāvaṅkatāni āyatiṁ anuppādadhammāni.
root_text.sn12.35:7.1: Avijjāya tveva, bhikkhu, asesavirāganirodhā yānissa tāni visūkāyikāni visevitāni vipphanditāni kānici kānici.
root_text.sn12.35:8.1: Avijjāya tveva, bhikkhu, asesavirāganirodhā yānissa tāni visūkāyikāni visevitāni vipphanditāni kānici kānici.
root_text.sn12.35:8.2: ‘Katame saṅkhārā, kassa ca panime saṅkhārā’ iti vā, ‘aññe saṅkhārā, aññassa ca panime saṅkhārā’ iti vā, ‘taṁ jīvaṁ taṁ sarīraṁ’ iti vā, ‘aññaṁ jīvaṁ, aññaṁ sarīraṁ’ iti vā.
root_text.sn12.35:8.3: Sabbānissa tāni pahīnāni bhavanti ucchinnamūlāni tālāvatthukatāni anabhāvaṅkatāni āyatiṁ anuppādadhammānī”ti.

translation_text.sn12.35:1.3: Choices are a requirement for consciousness. …
translation_text.sn12.35:1.4: That is how this entire mass of suffering originates.”
translation_text.sn12.35:1.5: When this was said, one of the mendicants asked the Buddha,
translation_text.sn12.35:1.6: “What are old age and death, sir, and who do they belong to?”
translation_text.sn12.35:1.7: “That’s not a cogent question,” said the Buddha. “You might say, ‘What are old age and death, and who do they belong to?’ Or you might say, ‘Old age and death are one thing, who they belong to is another.’ But both of these mean the same thing, only the phrasing differs.
translation_text.sn12.35:1.8: Mendicant, if you have the view that the soul and the body are one and the same, there is no living of the spiritual life.
translation_text.sn12.35:1.9: If you have the view that the soul is one thing, the body another, there is no living of the spiritual life.
translation_text.sn12.35:1.10: Avoiding these two extremes, the Realized One teaches by the middle way:
translation_text.sn12.35:1.11: ‘Rebirth is a requirement for old age and death.’”
translation_text.sn12.35:2.1: “What is rebirth, sir, and who does it belong to?”
translation_text.sn12.35:2.2: “That’s not a cogent question,” said the Buddha. “You might say, ‘What is rebirth, and who does it belong to?’ Or you might say, ‘Rebirth is one thing, who it belongs to is another.’ But both of these mean the same thing, only the phrasing differs.
translation_text.sn12.35:2.3: Mendicant, if you have the view that the soul and the body are one and the same, there is no living of the spiritual life.
translation_text.sn12.35:2.4: If you have the view that the soul is one thing, the body another, there is no living of the spiritual life.
translation_text.sn12.35:2.5: Avoiding these two extremes, the Realized One teaches by the middle way:
translation_text.sn12.35:2.6: ‘Continued existence is a requirement for rebirth.’”
translation_text.sn12.35:3.1: “What is continued existence, sir, and who is it for?”
translation_text.sn12.35:3.2: “That’s not a cogent question,” said the Buddha. “You might say, ‘What is continued existence, and who does it belong to?’ Or you might say, ‘Continued existence is one thing, who it belongs to is another.’ But both of these mean the same thing, only the phrasing differs.
translation_text.sn12.35:3.3: Mendicant, if you have the view that the soul and the body are one and the same, there is no living of the spiritual life.
translation_text.sn12.35:3.4: If you have the view that the soul is one thing, the body another, there is no living of the spiritual life.
translation_text.sn12.35:3.5: Avoiding these two extremes, the Realized One teaches by the middle way:
translation_text.sn12.35:3.6: ‘Grasping is a requirement for continued existence.’ …
translation_text.sn12.35:3.10: ‘The six sense fields are requirements for contact.’ …
translation_text.sn12.35:3.11: ‘Name and form are requirements for the six sense fields.’ …
translation_text.sn12.35:3.12: ‘Consciousness is a requirement for name and form.’ …
translation_text.sn12.35:4.1: “What are choices, sir, and who do they belong to?”
translation_text.sn12.35:4.2: “That’s not a cogent question,” said the Buddha. “You might say, ‘What are choices, and who do they belong to?’ Or you might say, ‘Choices are one thing, who they belong to is another.’ But both of these mean the same thing, only the phrasing differs.
translation_text.sn12.35:4.3: Mendicant, if you have the view that the soul and the body are one and the same, there is no living of the spiritual life.
translation_text.sn12.35:4.4: If you have the view that the soul is one thing, the body another, there is no living of the spiritual life.
translation_text.sn12.35:4.5: Avoiding these two extremes, the Realized One teaches by the middle way:
translation_text.sn12.35:5.1: When ignorance fades away and ceases with nothing left over, then any twists, ducks, and dodges are given up:
translation_text.sn12.35:5.2: ‘What are old age and death, and who do they belong to?’ or ‘old age and death are one thing, who they belong to is another’, or ‘the soul and the body are one and the same’, or ‘the soul is one thing, the body another.’
translation_text.sn12.35:5.3: These are all cut off at the root, made like a palm stump, obliterated, and unable to arise in the future.
translation_text.sn12.35:6.1: When ignorance fades away and ceases with nothing left over, then any twists, ducks, and dodges are given up:
translation_text.sn12.35:6.2: ‘What is rebirth, and who does it belong to?’ or ‘rebirth is one thing, who it belongs to is another’, or ‘the soul and the body are one and the same’, or ‘the soul is one thing, the body another.’
translation_text.sn12.35:6.3: These are all cut off at the root, made like a palm stump, obliterated, and unable to arise in the future.
translation_text.sn12.35:7.1: When ignorance fades away and ceases with nothing left over, then any twists, ducks, and dodges are given up:
translation_text.sn12.35:8.1: When ignorance fades away and ceases with nothing left over, then any twists, ducks, and dodges are given up:
translation_text.sn12.35:8.2: ‘What are choices, and who do they belong to?’ or ‘choices are one thing, who they belong to is another’, or ‘the soul and the body are one and the same’, or ‘the soul is one thing, the body another.’
translation_text.sn12.35:8.3: These are all cut off at the root, made like a palm stump, obliterated, and unable to arise in the future.”
